pi_items.source: Task description specifies this as the custom stream name the user must configure. Confirmed in task.json line 17.

OpenBCI Forum discussion confirmed that OpenBCI GUI streams TimeSeries data as LSL type 'EEG'. MATLAB code example from OpenBCI community uses 'type','EEG' to resolve streams. Task description specifies 'Data Type: TimeSeries (default)', which broadcasts as type 'EEG' over LSL.

File exists at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/openbci_gui_env/data/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt. Verified via direct file inspection. File has 15,250 data rows (samples) with 8 EEG channels at 250 Hz sample rate (61 seconds of data). File header confirms source as PhysioNet eegmmidb 1.0.0, Subject S001.

Task description requires LSL protocol for Networking widget (step 6). Task JSON specifies verification uses export_result.sh which runs LSL verification via pylsl library. Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 13) and export_result.sh (line 20).
setup_task.sh lines 13-16 install pylsl package. export_result.sh line 20 imports pylsl for LSL stream verification. Confirmed as the only external package required for verification.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real EEG data from PhysioNet's eegmmidb dataset (Subject S001, eyes-open baseline recording), converted from the original 64-channel 160Hz EDF+ format to an 8-channel 250Hz CSV format compatible with OpenBCI GUI playback. The LSL stream is configured to stream TimeSeries data as type 'EEG' per OpenBCI's implementation. Custom stream name 'OpenBCI_Station_A' is user-configurable. pylsl package is required for verification.
